title: الضوابط الأساسية للأمن السيبراني للبنية التحتية الحيوية
description: The Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Controls (ECC-CII) extend the NCA Essential Cybersecurity Controls to specifically address the operational technology (OT), industrial control systems (ICS), and SCADA environments of Saudi Arabia's critical national infrastructure, including oil and gas, power, water, and transport sectors. The framework establishes sector-specific cybersecurity requirements for operators of critical national infrastructure.

provisions.title: Article 1. Scope and Critical Infrastructure Sectors
provisions.content: The Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Controls apply to operators of critical national infrastructure in the following sectors: (1) Energy — oil and gas production, refining, petrochemical, electricity generation, transmission and distribution; (2) Water — water treatment, desalination, distribution and wastewater management; (3) Transport — airports, seaports, railways, and associated logistics infrastructure; (4) Healthcare — hospitals, pharmaceutical supply chains, and health data infrastructure; (5) Financial infrastructure — payment systems, stock exchanges, and clearing systems; (6) Government digital infrastructure — national identity systems, government data centres, and citizen-facing digital services. For the purposes of this framework, 'operational technology' (OT) means hardware and software that detects or causes changes through direct monitoring and/or control of industrial equipment, assets, processes and events. All covered entities shall conduct a criticality assessment to identify their most critical assets and apply enhanced controls to those assets.

provisions.title: Article 2. IT-OT Network Segmentation and Air-Gapping
provisions.content: Operators of critical infrastructure shall: (1) maintain strict network segmentation between IT (information technology) and OT (operational technology) networks; (2) implement a demilitarised zone (DMZ) for all data exchanges between IT and OT networks, using unidirectional security gateways (data diodes) where technically feasible; (3) not connect OT networks directly to the internet or to external networks without security controls approved by the NCA; (4) implement compensating controls where full IT-OT segmentation is not technically feasible, including enhanced monitoring, application whitelisting, and strict change management; (5) maintain an up-to-date inventory of all OT assets, including industrial control systems (ICS), SCADA systems, distributed control systems (DCS), and programmable logic controllers (PLCs); (6) implement OT-specific security monitoring using industrial protocol-aware sensors; (7) implement secure remote access for OT environments using jump servers and multi-factor authentication.

provisions.title: Article 3. Supply Chain Security for OT Environments
provisions.content: Operators of critical infrastructure shall implement supply chain security controls for OT equipment and software: (1) conduct security assessments of OT vendors and integrators before procurement; (2) verify the integrity of OT hardware and software before deployment, using vendor-provided attestations or independent verification; (3) implement a formal change management process for OT systems, including testing changes in a representative non-production environment before deployment; (4) not deploy OT hardware or software from vendors designated as posing a national security risk by the NCA or relevant government authority; (5) maintain original installation media and firmware for critical OT systems in secure offline storage; (6) implement OT-specific patch management processes taking into account the criticality and availability requirements of production systems; (7) audit OT vendor remote access sessions in real time, with session recording and the ability to terminate sessions immediately; (8) ensure that support contracts with OT vendors include security obligations equivalent to this framework.

provisions.title: Article 4. Industrial Incident Response
provisions.content: Operators of critical infrastructure shall develop and maintain OT-specific incident response capabilities: (1) develop an OT incident response plan separate from, but coordinated with, the IT incident response plan; (2) define procedures for safe degraded-mode operation and manual override of automated processes in the event of a cyberattack; (3) maintain and regularly test offline backups of OT configurations, programs, and historical data; (4) establish communication protocols with the NCA, the relevant sector regulator, and the National Centre for Critical Infrastructure Protection for significant OT incidents; (5) conduct tabletop exercises and OT-specific incident response drills at least annually; (6) report OT cybersecurity incidents to the NCA within 3 hours of detection; (7) preserve forensic evidence from OT systems following an incident in a manner that does not interfere with the safe resumption of operations; (8) cooperate with NCA investigations of OT incidents and accept NCA technical assistance.

provisions.title: Article 5. Physical Security Integration
provisions.content: Operators of critical infrastructure shall integrate cyber and physical security: (1) implement physical access controls for OT system components, including PLCs, HMIs, engineering workstations, and network equipment, with access restricted to authorised personnel; (2) implement CCTV surveillance in OT control rooms and critical equipment areas; (3) conduct background screening of personnel with physical or logical access to OT systems; (4) implement tamper-evident seals on critical OT hardware; (5) secure communications between field devices and control systems using encrypted industrial protocols where available and technically feasible; (6) implement physical security measures for communications infrastructure connecting OT sites, including substation protection and pipeline communications; (7) coordinate with the national physical security authorities on threats to critical infrastructure. Physical security incidents that may indicate a planned cyberattack shall be reported to the NCA.

provisions.title: Article 6. Compliance and Sector Regulators
provisions.content: Compliance with the ECC-CII shall be assessed by: (1) sector regulators designated by the NCA — for the energy sector, the Ministry of Energy; for water, the National Water Company; for transport, the General Authority of Civil Aviation (GACA) and the Saudi Ports Authority; (2) the NCA directly for cross-sector assessments; (3) independent assessors accredited by the NCA. Annual compliance reports shall be submitted to both the relevant sector regulator and the NCA. Non-compliance findings shall be remediated within timelines agreed with the sector regulator and the NCA. Operators of critical infrastructure failing to remediate significant vulnerabilities within the agreed timelines shall be subject to: sanctions imposed by the sector regulator; recommendations to the Council of Ministers; and in cases of deliberate non-compliance, referral to the competent prosecution authority. The NCA shall publish annually an aggregated assessment of the cybersecurity posture of Saudi Arabia's critical infrastructure.

definitions.definition: Hardware and software that detects or causes changes through direct monitoring and/or control of industrial equipment, assets, processes and events.

definitions.term: نظام التحكم الصناعي (Industrial Control System / ICS)
definitions.definition: Systems used for industrial process control, including SCADA systems, distributed control systems (DCS), and programmable logic controllers (PLCs).
Physical and digital infrastructure in designated sectors whose disruption would have significant consequences for the national security, economy, health, or safety of Saudi Arabia.
